message:
✨ Initial HA add-on: Tuya water quality monitor

Polls Tuya-based water quality testers (YINMIK) over the local network
via tinytuya and publishes TDS, pH, ORP, EC, temperature, and battery
to Home Assistant via MQTT auto-discovery. Supports protocol 3.1-3.5.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+# Water Quality Monitor
+
+Monitor Tuya-based water quality testers (like YINMIK) in Home Assistant via MQTT.
+
+## Sensors
+
+| Sensor | DPS | Unit | Description |
+|--------|-----|------|-------------|
+| TDS | 1 | ppm | Total Dissolved Solids |
+| EC | 2 | uS/cm | Electrical Conductivity |
+| pH | 7 | pH | Acidity/Alkalinity |
+| ORP | 10 | mV | Oxidation-Reduction Potential |
+| Conductivity | 11 | uS/cm | Conductivity |
+| Temperature | 12 | C | Water Temperature (raw / 10) |
+| Battery | 115 | % | Battery Level |
+
+## Configuration
+
+| Option | Description | Default |
+|--------|-------------|---------|
+| device_id | Tuya device ID | required |
+| device_ip | Device IP on your LAN | required |
+| local_key | Tuya local encryption key | required |
+| protocol_version | Tuya protocol (3.1-3.5) | 3.5 |
+| poll_interval_seconds | Seconds between polls | 30 |
+| mqtt_topic_prefix | MQTT topic prefix | water_quality |
+
+## Getting Device ID and Local Key
+
+1. Create a project on [Tuya IoT Platform](https://iot.tuya.com)
+2. Link your Tuya Smart / Smart Life app account
+3. Find your device under Devices
+4. Copy the Device ID and Local Key
+
+Or use `tinytuya wizard` locally:
+```bash
+pip install tinytuya
+python -m tinytuya wizard
+```
+
+## How It Works
+
+The add-on polls your Tuya water quality tester over the local network using tinytuya,
+then publishes all sensor readings to MQTT with Home Assistant auto-discovery.
+Sensors appear automatically in HA — no YAML configuration needed.

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+ARG BUILD_FROM
+FROM ${BUILD_FROM}
+
+RUN apk add --no-cache python3 py3-pip && \
+    pip3 install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages tinytuya paho-mqtt
+
+COPY run.sh /
+COPY water_quality.py /
+
+RUN chmod a+x /run.sh
+
+CMD [ "/run.sh" ]

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+# Water Quality Monitor for Home Assistant
+
+Home Assistant add-on that monitors Tuya-based water quality testers (TDS, pH, ORP, EC, temperature) via local network polling and MQTT.
+
+## Supported Devices
+
+- YINMIK Water Quality Tester (Tuya protocol 3.5)
+- Other Tuya-based water quality monitors with similar DPS mapping
+
+## Quick Start
+
+1. Copy this repository to `/addons/water-quality-monitor/` on your HA instance
+2. Go to **Settings > Add-ons > Add-on Store > Local add-ons** (three dots menu > Repositories)
+3. Install "Water Quality Monitor"
+4. Configure with your device ID, IP, and local key
+5. Start the add-on — sensors appear automatically via MQTT discovery
+
+## Installation via Repository
+
+Add this repository URL to your HA add-on store:
+
+```
+https://github.com/clubanderson/ha-water-quality-monitor
+```
+
+Then install "Water Quality Monitor" from the store.
+
+## Sensors
+
+- **TDS** (ppm) — Total Dissolved Solids
+- **pH** — Acidity/Alkalinity
+- **ORP** (mV) — Oxidation-Reduction Potential
+- **EC** (uS/cm) — Electrical Conductivity
+- **Temperature** (C)
+- **Battery** (%)
+
+See [DOCS.md](DOCS.md) for full configuration details.
+
+## License
+
+MIT

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+name: "Water Quality Monitor"
+description: "Monitor Tuya-based water quality testers (TDS, pH, ORP, EC, temperature) via MQTT"
+version: "1.0.0"
+slug: "water-quality-monitor"
+url: "https://github.com/clubanderson/ha-water-quality-monitor"
+arch:
+  - amd64
+  - aarch64
+  - armv7
+  - armhf
+  - i386
+startup: "application"
+boot: "auto"
+services:
+  - "mqtt:need"
+options:
+  device_id: ""
+  device_ip: ""
+  local_key: ""
+  protocol_version: "3.5"
+  poll_interval_seconds: 30
+  mqtt_topic_prefix: "water_quality"
+schema:
+  device_id: str
+  device_ip: str
+  local_key: str
+  protocol_version: list(3.1|3.2|3.3|3.4|3.5)
+  poll_interval_seconds: int(10,300)
+  mqtt_topic_prefix: str
+map:
+  - config:rw

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+#!/usr/bin/with-contenv bashio
+
+CONFIG_PATH=/data/options.json
+
+DEVICE_ID=$(bashio::config 'device_id')
+DEVICE_IP=$(bashio::config 'device_ip')
+LOCAL_KEY=$(bashio::config 'local_key')
+PROTOCOL_VERSION=$(bashio::config 'protocol_version')
+POLL_INTERVAL=$(bashio::config 'poll_interval_seconds')
+MQTT_PREFIX=$(bashio::config 'mqtt_topic_prefix')
+
+MQTT_HOST=$(bashio::services mqtt "host")
+MQTT_PORT=$(bashio::services mqtt "port")
+MQTT_USER=$(bashio::services mqtt "username")
+MQTT_PASS=$(bashio::services mqtt "password")
+
+bashio::log.info "Starting Water Quality Monitor"
+bashio::log.info "Device: ${DEVICE_ID} @ ${DEVICE_IP} (protocol ${PROTOCOL_VERSION})"
+bashio::log.info "MQTT: ${MQTT_HOST}:${MQTT_PORT} (prefix: ${MQTT_PREFIX})"
+
+exec python3 /water_quality.py \
+  --device-id "${DEVICE_ID}" \
+  --device-ip "${DEVICE_IP}" \
+  --local-key "${LOCAL_KEY}" \
+  --protocol "${PROTOCOL_VERSION}" \
+  --poll-interval "${POLL_INTERVAL}" \
+  --mqtt-host "${MQTT_HOST}" \
+  --mqtt-port "${MQTT_PORT}" \
+  --mqtt-user "${MQTT_USER}" \
+  --mqtt-pass "${MQTT_PASS}" \
+  --mqtt-prefix "${MQTT_PREFIX}"

@@ -0,0 +1,229 @@
+"""Water Quality Monitor — Tuya device to MQTT bridge for Home Assistant."""
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import logging
+import signal
+import sys
+import time
+
+import paho.mqtt.client as mqtt
+import tinytuya
+
+# Polling and retry constants
+RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS = 10
+CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
+MQTT_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS = 60
+
+logging.basicConfig(
+    level=logging.INFO,
+    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
+)
+log = logging.getLogger("water_quality")
+
+# DPS mapping for Tuya water quality testers (e.g., YINMIK)
+SENSORS = {
+    "1": {
+        "name": "TDS",
+        "unit": "ppm",
+        "device_class": None,
+        "icon": "mdi:water-opacity",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+    "2": {
+        "name": "EC",
+        "unit": "\u00b5S/cm",
+        "device_class": None,
+        "icon": "mdi:flash-outline",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+    "7": {
+        "name": "pH",
+        "unit": "pH",
+        "device_class": None,
+        "icon": "mdi:ph",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+    "10": {
+        "name": "ORP",
+        "unit": "mV",
+        "device_class": "voltage",
+        "icon": "mdi:lightning-bolt",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+    "11": {
+        "name": "Conductivity",
+        "unit": "\u00b5S/cm",
+        "device_class": None,
+        "icon": "mdi:flash-circle",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+    "12": {
+        "name": "Temperature",
+        "unit": "\u00b0C",
+        "device_class": "temperature",
+        "icon": "mdi:thermometer-water",
+        "scale": 0.1,
+    },
+    "115": {
+        "name": "Battery",
+        "unit": "%",
+        "device_class": "battery",
+        "icon": "mdi:battery",
+        "scale": 1,
+    },
+}
+
+running = True
+
+
+def signal_handler(_sig, _frame):
+    """Handle shutdown signals."""
+    global running
+    log.info("Shutting down...")
+    running = False
+
+
+signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, signal_handler)
+signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, signal_handler)
+
+
+def publish_discovery(mqtt_client, prefix, device_id):
+    """Publish MQTT auto-discovery messages for Home Assistant."""
+    device_info = {
+        "identifiers": [f"water_quality_{device_id}"],
+        "name": "Water Quality Tester",
+        "manufacturer": "YINMIK",
+        "model": "Tuya Water Quality Tester",
+        "sw_version": "1.0.0",
+    }
+
+    for dps_id, sensor in SENSORS.items():
+        slug = sensor["name"].lower().replace(" ", "_")
+        unique_id = f"water_quality_{device_id}_{slug}"
+        state_topic = f"{prefix}/{device_id}/state"
+
+        config_payload = {
+            "name": sensor["name"],
+            "unique_id": unique_id,
+            "state_topic": state_topic,
+            "value_template": f"{{{{ value_json.{slug} }}}}",
+            "unit_of_measurement": sensor["unit"],
+            "icon": sensor["icon"],
+            "device": device_info,
+        }
+
+        if sensor["device_class"]:
+            config_payload["device_class"] = sensor["device_class"]
+
+        discovery_topic = f"homeassistant/sensor/{unique_id}/config"
+        mqtt_client.publish(discovery_topic, json.dumps(config_payload), retain=True)
+        log.info("Published discovery for %s", sensor["name"])
+
+
+def poll_device(device):
+    """Poll the Tuya device and return scaled sensor values."""
+    data = device.status()
+
+    if "dps" not in data:
+        log.warning("No DPS in response: %s", data)
+        return None
+
+    dps = data["dps"]
+    values = {}
+
+    for dps_id, sensor in SENSORS.items():
+        raw = dps.get(dps_id)
+        if raw is not None:
+            slug = sensor["name"].lower().replace(" ", "_")
+            scaled = round(float(raw) * sensor["scale"], 2)
+            values[slug] = scaled
+
+    return values
+
+
+def main():
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Water Quality MQTT Bridge")
+    parser.add_argument("--device-id", required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--device-ip", required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--local-key", required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--protocol", default="3.5")
+    parser.add_argument("--poll-interval", type=int, default=30)
+    parser.add_argument("--mqtt-host", default="localhost")
+    parser.add_argument("--mqtt-port", type=int, default=1883)
+    parser.add_argument("--mqtt-user", default="")
+    parser.add_argument("--mqtt-pass", default="")
+    parser.add_argument("--mqtt-prefix", default="water_quality")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    # Connect to MQTT
+    mqtt_client = mqtt.Client(client_id="water_quality_monitor")
+    if args.mqtt_user:
+        mqtt_client.username_pw_set(args.mqtt_user, args.mqtt_pass)
+    mqtt_client.connect(args.mqtt_host, args.mqtt_port, MQTT_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS)
+    mqtt_client.loop_start()
+    log.info("Connected to MQTT at %s:%d", args.mqtt_host, args.mqtt_port)
+
+    # Connect to Tuya device
+    device = tinytuya.Device(args.device_id, args.device_ip, args.local_key)
+    device.set_version(float(args.protocol))
+    device.set_socketPersistent(True)
+    log.info(
+        "Connected to Tuya device %s at %s (protocol %s)",
+        args.device_id,
+        args.device_ip,
+        args.protocol,
+    )
+
+    # Publish HA MQTT auto-discovery
+    publish_discovery(mqtt_client, args.mqtt_prefix, args.device_id)
+
+    # Availability topic
+    avail_topic = f"{args.mqtt_prefix}/{args.device_id}/availability"
+    mqtt_client.publish(avail_topic, "online", retain=True)
+    state_topic = f"{args.mqtt_prefix}/{args.device_id}/state"
+
+    consecutive_failures = 0
+    MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 5
+
+    while running:
+        values = poll_device(device)
+
+        if values:
+            mqtt_client.publish(state_topic, json.dumps(values), retain=True)
+            log.info("Published: %s", values)
+            consecutive_failures = 0
+        else:
+            consecutive_failures += 1
+            log.warning(
+                "Poll failed (%d/%d)", consecutive_failures, MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES
+            )
+
+            if consecutive_failures >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES:
+                log.error("Too many failures, reconnecting device...")
+                mqtt_client.publish(avail_topic, "offline", retain=True)
+                try:
+                    device.close()
+                    time.sleep(RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS)
+                    device = tinytuya.Device(
+                        args.device_id, args.device_ip, args.local_key
+                    )
+                    device.set_version(float(args.protocol))
+                    device.set_socketPersistent(True)
+                    mqtt_client.publish(avail_topic, "online", retain=True)
+                    consecutive_failures = 0
+                except Exception as e:
+                    log.error("Reconnect failed: %s", e)
+
+        time.sleep(args.poll_interval)
+
+    # Clean shutdown
+    mqtt_client.publish(avail_topic, "offline", retain=True)
+    mqtt_client.loop_stop()
+    mqtt_client.disconnect()
+    device.close()
+    log.info("Shutdown complete")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
